Academic pricing is available: Student ($19.95/month for 3 users), Faculty ($94/month), Research Project ($194/month).

resources.title: EPPI-Reviewer: systematic review software
resources.description: EPPI-Reviewer is a web-based software for all types of literature reviews, including scoping reviews. It offers text mining features, machine learning for screening, and supports collaborative work. It can handle large-scale reviews with millions of items.

resources.title: EPPI-Reviewer Pricing
resources.description: EPPI-Reviewer uses a subscription model. A user fee is £10 per month for unlimited non-shareable reviews. A shareable review costs £35 per month per review, in addition to user fees for each collaborator. VAT is added for UK purchases. Minimum invoice purchase is £100, with a £25 surcharge for invoices under £500. Site licenses are also available.

resources.title: Nested Knowledge – The Standard in Evidence Synthesis
resources.description: Nested Knowledge is a comprehensive software platform for systematic literature review and meta-analysis. It features AutoLit for accelerated workflows (search, screening, extraction, appraisal) and Synthesis for visualizing, analyzing, publishing, and sharing insights. It includes AI-augmented screening and qualitative/quantitative synthesis capabilities.

resources.title: Nested Knowledge Pricing
resources.description: Nested Knowledge offers several pricing tiers: Business Essentials ($295/user/month for collaborative workflows), Enterprise ($695/user/month for Business Essentials + powerful AI), and Enterprise Plus (custom pricing for premium research support). Academic and student pricing is also available. Users can create their first Nest for free.

resources.title: SysRev: A modern take on document review
resources.description: SysRev is a platform for collaborative document review, offering AI-powered tools for data extraction. It supports various document types and complex data extraction, with adaptable workflows for multi-phase reviews. Pricing includes a free basic plan, a premium plan ($50/member/month), and a custom enterprise plan.

resources.title: Iris.ai Researcher Workspace
resources.description: Iris.ai Researcher Workspace offers AI-powered tools for streamlining literature reviews, including content-based search, context filtering, data filtering, data extraction, document set analysis, summarization, and chatting with datasets. It provides a 10-day free trial for individual membership and academic discounts. Enterprise editions are available with custom pricing.

resources.title: Elicit: AI for scientific research
resources.description: Elicit uses AI to automate parts of systematic and scoping reviews, including refining research questions, gathering sources, title & abstract screening, and data extraction. It provides detailed rationale for screening decisions and supports custom screening criteria. Pricing includes a free basic plan, a Pro plan ($49/user/month billed annually), a Scale plan ($169/user/month billed annually), and a custom Enterprise plan.

resources.title: Connected Papers: Find and explore academic papers
resources.description: Connected Papers is a visual tool that helps researchers find and explore academic papers relevant to their field by building a graph of similar papers based on citation networks. It helps get a visual overview of a new academic field, find missed important papers, create bibliographies, and discover relevant prior and derivative works. It offers a free plan (5 graphs/month), an Academic plan ($3/month billed annually), and a Business plan ($10/month billed annually).

resources.title: ResearchRabbit: AI Tool for Smarter, Faster Literature Reviews
resources.description: ResearchRabbit is an AI-powered tool for literature reviews, offering visual exploration, citation-based recommendations, and intelligent alerts. It helps researchers find related papers, build citation maps, and track research trends. It offers a free plan with unlimited searches and collections, and a ResearchRabbit+ plan ($10/month) for larger scale reviews with advanced search controls and multiple projects. Institutional plans are also available.

resources.title: Litmaps: Your Literature Review Assistant
resources.description: Litmaps is a literature review assistant that helps researchers discover, visualize, share, and monitor academic papers. It uses dynamic visualizations to help understand research trends and connections. It offers a free plan with limited features and a Pro plan (pricing not explicitly stated on the main pricing page, but mentioned as paid plans starting from $10/month in search results) with unlimited searches, maps, and advanced features like Zotero sync and customizable monitor alerts.

resources.title: Semantic Scholar: AI-Powered Research Tool
resources.description: Semantic Scholar is a free, AI-powered research tool that helps scholars discover relevant scientific literature. It offers features like semantic search, TLDRs (Too Long; Didn't Read) for quick summaries, highly influential citations, personalized research feeds, and alerts for new papers or citations. It also provides an online library to manage papers.

resources.title: Scoping Reviews - Writing Literature Reviews - Boston University Medical Library
resources.description: This guide focuses on scoping reviews for synthesizing evidence and assessing literature scope, useful for researchers in medical fields.

resources.title: Systematic Reviews and Other Evidence Synthesis Types - UT Southwestern Medical Center Library
resources.description: Provides an overview of scoping reviews as a preliminary assessment of research literature, distinguishing them from other evidence synthesis types.

resources.title: Scoping Review: Home - Binghamton University Libraries
resources.description: This guide introduces scoping reviews as an evidence synthesis method to systematically identify and map the breadth of evidence on a given topic.

resources.title: Scoping Reviews - Levy Library Research Synthesis and Systematic Reviews - Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
resources.description: Provides guidance for developing or reviewing scoping review protocols, serving as an educational tool for medical researchers.

resources.title: Scoping Reviews - Library Guides at James Cook University
resources.description: Outlines scoping reviews as a method to identify and overview research on a topic, referencing Arksey and O'Malley's methodological framework.

resources.title: Types of Reviews: Scoping Reviews (ScRs) - University of Kansas Medical Center Libraries
resources.description: Offers guidelines and standards for Scoping Reviews (ScRs), serving as a research guide for best practices in medical research.

resources.title: What is a Scoping Review? - Stony Brook University Libraries
resources.description: Defines scoping reviews as a preliminary assessment of available research literature, aiming to identify the nature and scope of evidence.

resources.title: Step 3: Conduct a Literature Search - Scoping & Systematic Reviews - Azusa Pacific University Libraries
resources.description: A guide on conducting literature searches for both scoping and systematic reviews, including librarian support for search strategy development.

resources.title: CVP Scoping Review Project - Emory University Libraries
resources.description: Focuses on a specific scoping review project related to cardiovascular topics, citing Munn et al. (2018) on the purpose of scoping reviews.

resources.title: Scoping Reviews - Scholarly Publishing - University of North Dakota Libraries
resources.description: Highlights the librarian's role in supporting scoping reviews, from translating research questions to locating existing systematic reviews.

resources.title: Conducting a Scoping Review - Galter Health Sciences Library, Northwestern University
resources.description: This class provides an overview of scoping reviews and the process of conducting one, offered online via Zoom.

resources.title: Scoping Reviews: Workshops & Training - Ontario Tech University Library
resources.description: This session explores the distinctions between scoping reviews and systematic reviews, and necessary adjustments one needs to make in the setup and searching.

resources.title: Systematic Review and Scoping Review Tutorials - Washington University in St. Louis, Becker Medical Library
resources.description: Tutorials designed to help researchers better understand how to complete an evidence synthesis project, including systematic and scoping reviews.

resources.title: JBI Scoping Review Workshop - University of Nottingham
resources.description: An online JBI Scoping Review Workshop offered by the University of Nottingham, covering theories and concepts of scoping reviews.

resources.title: 5th Annual - Winter Institute for Scoping Reviews - Rutgers School of Nursing
resources.description: Provides hands-on instruction and practice using JBI SUMARI software for scoping reviews, aimed at advanced researchers in nursing and public health.

resources.title: JBI Scoping Review workshop (online) - Cardiff University
resources.description: An online workshop from Cardiff University exploring theories and concepts related to scoping reviews and other types of evidence synthesis.

resources.title: JBI Scoping Review Workshop - March 2026 - Humanitix
resources.description: A two-day JBI Scoping Review Workshop designed for clinicians, public health professionals, academics, and researchers.

resources.title: Evidence Synthesis Classes - NIH Library
resources.description: Training that presents frameworks like PICO for systematic reviews and PCC for scoping reviews, along with other lesser-known frameworks.

resources.title: Evidence Syntheses (Scoping, systematic, & other types of reviews) - University of Michigan Library
resources.description: This guide covers various evidence syntheses, from highly systematic to less formal methodologies, providing a broad overview for researchers.

resources.title: Evidence Synthesis Guide : Training Resources - Mayo Clinic Libraries
resources.description: Provides information and resources for undertaking systematic reviews, scoping reviews, or other types of evidence synthesis, with a focus on training.

resources.title: Practical, evidence-based guidance to support implementation in aged care
resources.description: Guidance on implementing evidence-based practices in aged care.

resources.title: Tips on submitting to the JBI Evidence Synthesis journal
resources.description: Tips for researchers looking to submit their work to the JBI Evidence Synthesis journal.

resources.title: Barriers and enablers in health care
resources.description: Discussion on barriers and enablers affecting healthcare implementation.

resources.title: Risk of bias for analytical cross-sectional studies
resources.description: Explaining risk of bias in analytical cross-sectional studies.

resources.title: JBI Talking Evidence 04: Talking Implementation - Practice change and sustainability
resources.description: JBI Talking Evidence episode on practice change and sustainability in implementation.

resources.title: JBI Talking Evidence 03: Talking Implementation - Top tips for reliable data collection
resources.description: JBI Talking Evidence episode on top tips for reliable data collection in implementation.

resources.title: JBI SUMARI Tutorial: Critical appraisal
resources.description: A tutorial on critical appraisal using JBI SUMARI software.

resources.title: Pre-planning and protocol development for systematic reviews
resources.description: Guidance on pre-planning and protocol development for systematic reviews.

resources.title: What is Methodology?
resources.description: An introductory video explaining what methodology is.

resources.title: Should I undertake a scoping review or a systematic review?
resources.description: A video discussing the differences between scoping reviews and systematic reviews to help in decision-making.

resources.title: JBI Model of EBHC explained in English
resources.description: Explanation of the JBI Model of Evidence-Based Healthcare in English.

resources.title: Searching for studies: Basics of a systematic search
resources.description: Basics of conducting a systematic search for studies.

resources.title: JBI COnNECT+
resources.description: JBI COnNECT+ is a clinical online network providing easy access to evidence-based resources for clinical decision-making. While it offers resources, its community aspect seems limited to resource sharing rather than interactive forums.

resources.title: Evidence Synthesis Infrastructure Collaborative (ESIC)
resources.description: A 'community of communities' focused on improving evidence synthesis through collaboration, infrastructure development, and AI-enabled tools. It brings together various stakeholders including UN entities, major evidence synthesis producers (Cochrane, Campbell, JBI), and funders.

resources.title: Cochrane Engage
resources.description: Platform for volunteers to offer time and skills to health researchers for various research tasks within Cochrane.

resources.title: ACRL Evidence Synthesis Methods Interest Group
resources.description: An interest group focused on promoting and developing competencies in evidence synthesis methods, including scoping reviews. It offers a discussion list through ALA Connect for peer-support and resource sharing.

resources.title: Evidence Synthesis Taxonomy Initiative Mailing List
resources.description: Mailing list for an international community interested in evidence synthesis methods and methodologies.

resources.title: Campbell Collaboration
resources.description: A global team of researchers and policymakers focused on producing systematic reviews and evidence syntheses to inform economic and social policy decisions. It includes specialist coordinating groups and regional centers.

resources.title: #ScopingReviews Twitter/X Community
resources.description: A hashtag community on Twitter/X where researchers and practitioners discuss and share content related to scoping reviews.

resources.title: Evidence Synthesis Ireland (@EvidSynIRL) Twitter/X Account
resources.description: The official Twitter/X account for Evidence Synthesis Ireland, which shares updates, courses, and fosters discussions around evidence synthesis.
